# frozen_string_literal: true

# Redmine - project management software
# Copyright (C) 2006-  Jean-Philippe Lang
#
# This program is free software; you can redistribute it and/or
# modify it under the terms of the GNU General Public License
# as published by the Free Software Foundation; either version 2
# of the License, or (at your option) any later version.
#
# This program is distributed in the hope that it will be useful,
# but WITHOUT ANY WARRANTY; without even the implied warranty of
# MERCHANTABILITY or FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE.  See the
# GNU General Public License for more details.
#
# You should have received a copy of the GNU General Public License
# along with this program; if not, write to the Free Software
# Foundation, Inc., 51 Franklin Street, Fifth Floor, Boston, MA  02110-1301, USA.

class Issue < ApplicationRecord
  include Redmine::SafeAttributes
  include Redmine::Utils::DateCalculation
  include Redmine::I18n

  before_validation :default_assign, on: :create
  before_validation :force_default_value_on_noneditable_custom_fields, on: :create
  before_validation :clear_disabled_fields
  before_save :set_parent_id
  include Redmine::NestedSet::IssueNestedSet
  include Redmine::Reaction::Reactable

  belongs_to :project
  belongs_to :tracker
  belongs_to :status, :class_name => 'IssueStatus'
  belongs_to :author, :class_name => 'User'
  belongs_to :assigned_to, :class_name => 'Principal'
  belongs_to :fixed_version, :class_name => 'Version'
  belongs_to :priority, :class_name => 'IssuePriority'
  belongs_to :category, :class_name => 'IssueCategory'

  has_many :journals, :as => :journalized, :dependent => :destroy, :inverse_of => :journalized
  has_many :time_entries, :dependent => :destroy
  has_and_belongs_to_many :changesets, lambda {order("#{Changeset.table_name}.committed_on ASC, #{Changeset.table_name}.id ASC")}

  has_many :relations_from, :class_name => 'IssueRelation', :foreign_key => 'issue_from_id', :dependent => :delete_all
  has_many :relations_to, :class_name => 'IssueRelation', :foreign_key => 'issue_to_id', :dependent => :delete_all

  acts_as_attachable :after_add => :attachment_added, :after_remove => :attachment_removed
  acts_as_customizable
  acts_as_watchable
  acts_as_searchable :columns => ['subject', "#{table_name}.description"],
                     :preload => [:project, :status, :tracker],
                     :scope => lambda {|options| options[:open_issues] ? self.open : self.all}

  acts_as_event :title => Proc.new {|o| "#{o.tracker.name} ##{o.id} (#{o.status}): #{o.subject}"},
                :url => Proc.new {|o| {:controller => 'issues', :action => 'show', :id => o.id}},
                :type => Proc.new {|o| 'issue' + (o.closed? ? '-closed' : '')}

  acts_as_activity_provider :scope => proc {preload(:project, :author, :tracker, :status)},
                            :author_key => :author_id

  acts_as_mentionable :attributes => ['description']
  acts_as_webhookable
  include Issue::Webhookable

  DONE_RATIO_OPTIONS = %w(issue_field issue_status)

  attr_reader :transition_warning
  attr_writer :deleted_attachment_ids
  delegate :notes, :notes=, :private_notes, :private_notes=, :to => :current_journal, :allow_nil => true

  validates_presence_of :subject, :project, :tracker
  validates_presence_of :priority, :if => Proc.new {|issue| issue.new_record? || issue.priority_id_changed?}
  validates_presence_of :status, :if => Proc.new {|issue| issue.new_record? || issue.status_id_changed?}
  validates_presence_of :author, :if => Proc.new {|issue| issue.new_record? || issue.author_id_changed?}

  validates_length_of :subject, :maximum => 255
  validates_inclusion_of :done_ratio, :in => 0..100
  validates :estimated_hours, :numericality => {:greater_than_or_equal_to => 0, :allow_nil => true, :message => :invalid}
  validates :start_date, :date => true
  validates :due_date, :date => true
  validate :validate_issue, :validate_required_fields, :validate_permissions

  scope :visible, (lambda do |*args|
    joins(:project).
    where(Issue.visible_condition(args.shift || User.current, *args))
  end)

  scope :open, (lambda do |*args|
    is_closed = !args.empty? ? !args.first : false
    joins(:status).
    where(:issue_statuses => {:is_closed => is_closed})
  end)

  scope :recently_updated, lambda {order(:updated_on => :desc)}
  scope :on_active_project, (lambda do
    joins(:project).
    where(:projects => {:status => Project::STATUS_ACTIVE})
  end)
  scope :fixed_version, (lambda do |versions|
    ids = [versions].flatten.compact.map {|v| v.is_a?(Version) ? v.id : v}
    ids.any? ? where(:fixed_version_id => ids) : none
  end)
  scope :assigned_to, (lambda do |arg|
    arg = Array(arg).uniq
    ids = arg.map {|p| p.is_a?(Principal) ? p.id : p}
    ids += arg.grep(User).map(&:group_ids).flatten.uniq
    ids.compact!
    ids.any? ? where(:assigned_to_id => ids) : none
  end)
  scope :like, (lambda do |q|
    if q.present?
      where(*::Query.tokenized_like_conditions("#{table_name}.subject", q))
    end
  end)

  before_save :close_duplicates, :update_done_ratio_from_issue_status,
              :force_updated_on_change, :update_closed_on
  after_save do |issue|
    if !issue.saved_change_to_id? && issue.saved_change_to_project_id?

after_save do |issue|
    if !issue.saved_change_to_id? && issue.saved_change_to_project_id?
      issue.send :after_project_change
    end
  end
  after_destroy :update_parent_attributes
  after_save :reschedule_following_issues, :update_nested_set_attributes,
             :update_parent_attributes, :delete_selected_attachments, :create_journal
  # Should be after_create but would be called before previous after_save callbacks
  after_save :after_create_from_copy
  # add_auto_watcher needs to run before sending notifications, thus it needs
  # to be added after send_notification (after_ callbacks are run in inverse order)
  # https://api.rubyonrails.org/v5.2.3/classes/ActiveSupport/Callbacks/ClassMethods.html#method-i-set_callback
  after_create_commit :send_notification
  after_create_commit :add_auto_watcher
  after_commit :create_parent_issue_journal

  # Returns a SQL conditions string used to find all issues visible by the specified user
  def self.visible_condition(user, options={})
    Project.allowed_to_condition(user, :view_issues, options) do |role, user|
      sql =
        if user.id && user.logged?
          case role.issues_visibility
          when 'all'
            '1=1'
          when 'default'
            user_ids = [user.id] + user.groups.pluck(:id).compact
            "(#{table_name}.is_private = #{connection.quoted_false} " \
              "OR #{table_name}.author_id = #{user.id} " \
              "OR #{table_name}.assigned_to_id IN (#{user_ids.join(',')}))"
          when 'own'
            user_ids = [user.id] + user.groups.pluck(:id).compact
            "(#{table_name}.author_id = #{user.id} OR " \
              "#{table_name}.assigned_to_id IN (#{user_ids.join(',')}))"
          else
            '1=0'
          end
        else
          "(#{table_name}.is_private = #{connection.quoted_false})"
        end
      unless role.permissions_all_trackers?(:view_issues)
        tracker_ids = role.permissions_tracker_ids(:view_issues)
        if tracker_ids.any?
          sql = "(#{sql} AND #{table_name}.tracker_id IN (#{tracker_ids.join(',')}))"
        else
          sql = '1=0'
        end
      end
      sql
    end
  end

  # Returns true if usr or current user is allowed to view the issue
  def visible?(usr=nil)
    (usr || User.current).allowed_to?(:view_issues, self.project) do |role, user|
      visible =
        if user.logged?
          case role.issues_visibility
          when 'all'
            true
          when 'default'
            !self.is_private? || (self.author == user || user.is_or_belongs_to?(assigned_to))
          when 'own'
            self.author == user || user.is_or_belongs_to?(assigned_to)
          else
            false
          end
        else
          !self.is_private?
        end
      unless role.permissions_all_trackers?(:view_issues)
        visible &&= role.permissions_tracker_ids?(:view_issues, tracker_id)
      end
      visible
    end
  end

  # Returns true if user or current user is allowed to log time on the issue
  def time_loggable?(user=User.current)
    user.allowed_to?(:log_time, project) && (Setting.timelog_accept_closed_issues? || !closed?)
  end

  # Returns true if user or current user is allowed to edit or add notes to the issue
  def editable?(user=User.current)
    attributes_editable?(user) || notes_addable?(user)
  end

  # Returns true if user or current user is allowed to edit the issue
  def attributes_editable?(user=User.current)
    user_tracker_permission?(user, :edit_issues) || (
      user_tracker_permission?(user, :edit_own_issues) && author == user
    )
  end

  def attachments_addable?(user=User.current)
    attributes_editable?(user) || notes_addable?(user)
  end

  # Overrides Redmine::Acts::Attachable::InstanceMethods#attachments_editable?
  def attachments_editable?(user=User.current)
    visible?(user) && attributes_editable?(user)
  end

  # Returns true if user or current user is allowed to add notes to the issue
  def notes_addable?(user=User.current)
    user_tracker_permission?(user, :add_issue_notes)
  end

  # Returns true if user or current user is allowed to delete the issue
  def deletable?(user=User.current)
    user_tracker_permission?(user, :delete_issues)
  end

  # Overrides Redmine::Acts::Attachable::InstanceMethods#attachments_deletable?
  def attachments_deletable?(user=User.current)
    visible?(user) && attributes_editable?(user)
  end

  def initialize(attributes=nil, *args)
    super

def destroy
    super
  rescue ActiveRecord::StaleObjectError, ActiveRecord::RecordNotFound
    # Stale or already deleted
    begin
      reload
    rescue ActiveRecord::RecordNotFound
      # The issue was actually already deleted
      @destroyed = true
      return freeze
    end
    # The issue was stale, retry to destroy
    super
  end

  alias :base_reload :reload
  def reload(*)
    @workflow_rule_by_attribute = nil
    @assignable_versions = nil
    @relations = nil
    @spent_hours = nil
    @total_spent_hours = nil
    @total_estimated_hours = nil
    @last_updated_by = nil
    @last_notes = nil
    base_reload(*)
  end

  # Overrides Redmine::Acts::Customizable::InstanceMethods#available_custom_fields
  def available_custom_fields
    (project && tracker) ? (project.all_issue_custom_fields & tracker.custom_fields) : []
  end

  def visible_custom_field_values(user=nil)
    user_real = user || User.current
    custom_field_values.select do |value|
      value.custom_field.visible_by?(project, user_real)
    end
  end

  # Overrides Redmine::Acts::Customizable::InstanceMethods#set_custom_field_default?
  def set_custom_field_default?(custom_value)
    new_record? || project_id_changed?|| tracker_id_changed?
  end

  # Copies attributes from another issue, arg can be an id or an Issue
  def copy_from(arg, options={})
    issue = arg.is_a?(Issue) ? arg : Issue.visible.find(arg)
    self.attributes =
      issue.attributes.dup.except(
        "id", "root_id", "parent_id", "lft", "rgt",
        "created_on", "updated_on", "status_id", "closed_on"
      )
    self.custom_field_values =
      issue.custom_field_values.to_h do |v|
        [v.custom_field_id, v.value]
      end
    if options[:keep_status]
      self.status = issue.status
    end
    self.author = User.current
    unless options[:attachments] == false
      self.attachments = issue.attachments.map do |attachement|
        attachement.copy(:container => self)
      end
    end

    unless options[:watchers] == false
      self.watcher_user_ids = issue.visible_watcher_users.select{|u| u.status == User::STATUS_ACTIVE}.map(&:id)
    end
    @copied_from = issue
    @copy_options = options
    self
  end

  # Returns an unsaved copy of the issue
  def copy(attributes=nil, copy_options={})
    copy = self.class.new.copy_from(self, copy_options)
    copy.attributes = attributes if attributes
    copy
  end

  # Returns true if the issue is a copy
  def copy?
    @copied_from.present?
  end

  def status_id=(status_id)
    if status_id.to_s != self.status_id.to_s
      self.status = (status_id.present? ? IssueStatus.find_by_id(status_id) : nil)

def destroy
    raise Unauthorized unless @issues.all?(&:deletable?)

    # all issues and their descendants are about to be deleted
    issues_and_descendants_ids = Issue.self_and_descendants(@issues).pluck(:id)
    time_entries = TimeEntry.where(:issue_id => issues_and_descendants_ids)
    @hours = time_entries.sum(:hours).to_f

    if @hours > 0
      case params[:todo]
      when 'destroy'
        # nothing to do
      when 'nullify'
        if Setting.timelog_required_fields.include?('issue_id')
          flash.now[:error] = l(:field_issue) + " " + ::I18n.t('activerecord.errors.messages.blank')
          return
        else
          time_entries.update_all(:issue_id => nil)
        end
      when 'reassign'
        reassign_to = @project && @project.issues.find_by_id(params[:reassign_to_id])
        if reassign_to.nil?
          flash.now[:error] = l(:error_issue_not_found_in_project)
          return
        elsif issues_and_descendants_ids.include?(reassign_to.id)
          flash.now[:error] = l(:error_cannot_reassign_time_entries_to_an_issue_about_to_be_deleted)
          return
        else
          time_entries.update_all(:issue_id => reassign_to.id, :project_id => reassign_to.project_id)
        end
      else
        # display the destroy form if it's a user request
        return unless api_request?
      end
    end
    @issues.each do |issue|
      begin
        issue.reload.destroy
      rescue ::ActiveRecord::RecordNotFound # raised by #reload if issue no longer exists
        # nothing to do, issue was already deleted (eg. by a parent)
      end
    end
    respond_to do |format|
      format.html do
        flash[:notice] = l(:notice_successful_delete)
        redirect_back_or_default _project_issues_path(@project)
      end
      format.api  {render_api_ok}
    end
  end

  # Overrides Redmine::MenuManager::MenuController::ClassMethods for
  # when the "New issue" tab is enabled
  def current_menu_item
    if Setting.new_item_menu_tab == '1' && [:new, :create].include?(action_name.to_sym)

  # Called after_save
  def create_journal
    if current_journal
      current_journal.save
    end
  end

  def create_parent_issue_journal
    return if persisted? && !saved_change_to_parent_id?
    return if destroyed? && @without_nested_set_update

    child_id = self.id
    old_parent_id, new_parent_id =
      if persisted?
        [parent_id_before_last_save, parent_id]
      elsif destroyed?
        [parent_id, nil]
      else
        [nil, parent_id]
      end

    if old_parent_id.present?
      Issue.transaction do
        if old_parent_issue = Issue.visible.lock.find_by_id(old_parent_id)
          old_parent_issue.init_journal(User.current)
          old_parent_issue.current_journal.__send__(:add_attribute_detail, 'child_id', child_id, nil)
          old_parent_issue.save
        end
      end
    end

    if new_parent_id.present?
      Issue.transaction do
        if new_parent_issue = Issue.visible.lock.find_by_id(new_parent_id)
          new_parent_issue.init_journal(User.current)
          new_parent_issue.current_journal.__send__(:add_attribute_detail, 'child_id', nil, child_id)
          new_parent_issue.save
        end
      end
    end
  end

  def add_auto_watcher
    if author&.active? &&
        author.allowed_to?(:add_issue_watchers, project) &&
        author.pref.auto_watch_on?('issue_created') &&
        self.watcher_user_ids.exclude?(author.id)
      self.set_watcher(author, true)
    end
  end

  def send_notification
    if notify? && Setting.notified_events.include?('issue_added')
      Mailer.deliver_issue_add(self)
    end
  end

  def clear_disabled_fields
    if tracker
      tracker.disabled_core_fields.each do |attribute|
        send :"#{attribute}=", nil
      end

  def force_default_value_on_noneditable_custom_fields
    return unless custom_field_values_changed?

    custom_field_values.each do |field_value|

  end

end

  # Called after_save
  def create_journal
    if current_journal
      current_journal.save
    end
  end

  def create_parent_issue_journal
    return if persisted? && !saved_change_to_parent_id?
    return if destroyed? && @without_nested_set_update

    child_id = self.id
    old_parent_id, new_parent_id =
      if persisted?
        [parent_id_before_last_save, parent_id]
      elsif destroyed?
        [parent_id, nil]
      else
        [nil, parent_id]
      end

    if old_parent_id.present?
      Issue.transaction do
        if old_parent_issue = Issue.visible.lock.find_by_id(old_parent_id)
          old_parent_issue.init_journal(User.current)
          old_parent_issue.current_journal.__send__(:add_attribute_detail, 'child_id', child_id, nil)
          old_parent_issue.save
        end
      end
    end

    if new_parent_id.present?
      Issue.transaction do
        if new_parent_issue = Issue.visible.lock.find_by_id(new_parent_id)
          new_parent_issue.init_journal(User.current)
          new_parent_issue.current_journal.__send__(:add_attribute_detail, 'child_id', nil, child_id)
          new_parent_issue.save
        end
      end
    end
  end

  def add_auto_watcher
    if author&.active? &&
        author.allowed_to?(:add_issue_watchers, project) &&
        author.pref.auto_watch_on?('issue_created') &&
        self.watcher_user_ids.exclude?(author.id)
      self.set_watcher(author, true)
    end
  end

  def send_notification
    if notify? && Setting.notified_events.include?('issue_added')
      Mailer.deliver_issue_add(self)
    end
  end

def issues
        @issues ||= @query.issues(
          :order => ["#{Project.table_name}.lft ASC", "#{Issue.table_name}.id ASC"],
          :limit => @max_rows
        )
      end

      # Returns a hash of the relations between the issues that are present on the gantt
      # and that should be displayed, grouped by issue ids.
      def relations
        return @relations if @relations

        if issues.any?
          issue_ids = issues.map(&:id)
          @relations = IssueRelation.
            where(:issue_from_id => issue_ids, :issue_to_id => issue_ids, :relation_type => DRAW_TYPES.keys).
            group_by(&:issue_from_id)
        else
          @relations = {}
        end
      end

      # Return all the project nodes that will be displayed
      def projects
        return @projects if @projects

        ids = issues.collect(&:project).uniq.collect(&:id)
        if ids.any?
          # All issues projects and their visible ancestors
          @projects = Project.visible.

content.text:
I now have a complete picture. Here is the full audit.

---

## Step 1 — Orientation

**Project.** Redmine, a Ruby on Rails project-management web app (`README.rdoc:1`). Standard Rails MVC layout under `app/`:
- `app/models/` — ActiveRecord models (`issue.rb`, `journal.rb`, `time_entry.rb`, `project.rb`, …)
- `app/controllers/`, `app/helpers/`, `app/views/`, `app/jobs/` (ActiveJob async workers)
- `lib/redmine/` — core framework code (helpers, nested set, search, export, i18n, hooks)
- `lib/plugins/` — vendored "acts_as_*" mixins that Issue depends on
- `config/`, `db/`, `test/` (unit + functional), `plugins/` (external plugins)

**The central record** is **`Issue`** at `app/models/issue.rb:20` (2151 lines) — what people create, comment on (journals), log time against, link, and report on. The shared pieces it is built on:
- Concerns included directly: `Redmine::SafeAttributes`, `Redmine::Utils::DateCalculation`, `Redmine::I18n` (`issue.rb:21-23`), `Redmine::NestedSet::IssueNestedSet` (`issue.rb:29`, file `lib/redmine/nested_set/issue_nested_set.rb`), `Redmine::Reaction::Reactable` (`issue.rb:30`, file `lib/redmine/reaction.rb`)
- "acts_as" mixins in `lib/plugins/*/lib/`: attachable, customizable, watchable, searchable, event, activity_provider (`issue.rb:48-60`), plus mentionable/webhookable in `lib/redmine/acts/` (`issue.rb:62-64`)
- Per-model concern `Issue::Webhookable` at `app/models/concerns/issue/webhookable.rb:20`

---

## Step 2 — The Issue contract and what it's built on

**Model:** `app/models/issue.rb:20`.

**Behaviors it acts as (each pulls in callbacks, associations, scopes, instance methods):**
- `include Redmine::SafeAttributes` — `issue.rb:21` → mass-assignment whitelist (`safe_attributes` declarations later in file)
- `include Redmine::Utils::DateCalculation` — `issue.rb:22` → `working_days`, used by rescheduling
- `include Redmine::I18n` — `issue.rb:23`
- `include Redmine::NestedSet::IssueNestedSet` — `issue.rb:29` (`lib/redmine/nested_set/issue_nested_set.rb`) → `lft/rgt/root_id` tree, `parent`/`children`, `self_and_descendants`; overrides save/destroy ordering
- `include Redmine::Reaction::Reactable` — `issue.rb:30` (`lib/redmine/reaction.rb:39`) → `has_many :reactions, as: :reactable, dependent: :delete_all`
- `acts_as_attachable :after_add => :attachment_added, :after_remove => :attachment_removed` — `issue.rb:48` (`lib/plugins/acts_as_attachable`)
- `acts_as_customizable` — `issue.rb:49` (`lib/plugins/acts_as_customizable`) → `custom_values`/`custom_field_values`
- `acts_as_watchable` — `issue.rb:50` (`lib/plugins/acts_as_watchable`) → `has_many :watchers`, `notified_watchers`
- `acts_as_searchable` — `issue.rb:51-53`
- `acts_as_event` — `issue.rb:55-57` (activity feed title/url/type)
- `acts_as_activity_provider` — `issue.rb:59-60`
- `acts_as_mentionable :attributes => ['description']` — `issue.rb:62` (`lib/redmine/acts/mentionable.rb`)
- `acts_as_webhookable` + `include Issue::Webhookable` — `issue.rb:63-64`

**Associations other records hang off (the teardown surface):**
- `belongs_to :project/:tracker/:status/:author/:assigned_to/:fixed_version/:priority/:category` — `issue.rb:32-39`
- `has_many :journals, as: :journalized, dependent: :destroy` — `issue.rb:41`
- `has_many :time_entries, dependent: :destroy` — `issue.rb:42`
- `has_and_belongs_to_many :changesets` — `issue.rb:43`
- `has_many :relations_from / :relations_to (IssueRelation), dependent: :delete_all` — `issue.rb:45-46`
- watchers/reactions/attachments/custom_values via the acts mixins above

**What a teammate must know before changing it:** the destroy/teardown contract is spread across **four different dependent strategies** — `:destroy` (journals, time_entries — runs callbacks), `:delete_all` (relations — skips callbacks), `:delete_all` (reactions), and HABTM join rows (changesets, **not** the changesets themselves). Validation is heavy (`validate_issue, validate_required_fields, validate_permissions` — `issue.rb:82`), and visibility is contract-level: `self.visible_condition(user,…)` (`issue.rb:137`) and `visible?` (`issue.rb:172`) are consumed by many external subsystems (Step 5).

---

## Step 3 — Change lifecycle and fan-out

**Trigger points (request handling):**
- Create: `IssuesController#create` (`issues_controller.rb:151`) → `build_new_issue_from_params` (`:592`) → `save_issue_with_child_records` inside `Issue.transaction` (`:668`)
- Update: `#update` (`:198`) → `update_issue_from_params` → `@issue.init_journal(User.current)` (`:566`)
- Destroy: `#destroy` (`:431`) → `issue.reload.destroy` per issue (`:468`)

**Model callback fan-out (`issue.rb`):**
- `before_save :set_parent_id` (`:28`), `:close_duplicates, :update_done_ratio_from_issue_status, :force_updated_on_change, :update_closed_on` (`:117-118`)
- `after_save :reschedule_following_issues, :update_nested_set_attributes, :update_parent_attributes, :delete_selected_attachments, :create_journal` (`:125-126`) — **all inline**
- `after_destroy :update_parent_attributes` (`:124`) — parent/child rollup recompute, inline
- `after_save :after_create_from_copy` (`:128`) — copy linked relations/watchers inline
- `after_create_commit :send_notification` (`:132`) and `:add_auto_watcher` (`:133`)
- `after_commit :create_parent_issue_journal` (`:134`) — journals the parent on reparent/destroy (`:2054`)

**Effects by layer:**
- **History/journals:** `create_journal` (`:2049`) saves `current_journal` inline; `create_parent_issue_journal` (`:2054`) writes a child_id detail to the parent. Journal model fires `after_create_commit :send_notification` (`journal.rb:66`).
- **Notifications:** new-issue mail via `send_notification` → `Mailer.deliver_issue_add` (`issue.rb:2103`, `mailer.rb:105`); update mail via `Journal#send_notification` → `Mailer.deliver_issue_edit` (`journal.rb:374,385`, `mailer.rb:141`). Both call `.deliver_later` (`mailer.rb:108`) — **async**.
- **Time entries:** on destroy, controller pre-handles `destroy/nullify/reassign` (`issues_controller.rb:436-460`) before `dependent: :destroy` removes the rest — inline.
- **Linked changesets:** HABTM join cleared on destroy; changesets created elsewhere call `scan_for_issues`/`fix_issue`/`log_time` (`changeset.rb:64,108,154-155`).
- **Relations:** `relations_from/to` deleted via `delete_all` (`issue.rb:45-46`) — inline, no callbacks.
- **Parent/child rollups:** `update_parent_attributes` → `recalculate_attributes_for` (`issue.rb:1845,1863`) recomputes done_ratio/hours/dates; `reschedule_following_issues` (`:2000`) cascades dates to successors — inline.
- **Webhooks:** `acts_as_webhookable` enqueues `WebhookJob#perform` (`app/jobs/webhook_job.rb:21`) — **async**.
- **Async project teardown:** `DestroyProjectJob` → `project.destroy` (`destroy_project_job.rb:48`) cascades `has_many :issues, dependent: :destroy` (`project.rb:40`) — **async**.

---

## Step 4 — Every dependent of the Issue contract

### A. Request handling (controllers)
- `app/controllers/issues_controller.rb` — full CRUD/bulk/destroy of issues
- `app/controllers/issue_relations_controller.rb` — creates/deletes `IssueRelation` on issues
- `app/controllers/issue_categories_controller.rb` — reassigns issues on category delete
- `app/controllers/issue_statuses_controller.rb` — status lifecycle used by issues
- `app/controllers/context_menus_controller.rb:27` — bulk context menu over selected issues (`Issue.allowed_target_projects/trackers`, `:38,:45`)
- `app/controllers/auto_completes_controller.rb:23` — issue autocomplete (`Issue.cross_project_scope(...).visible`, `:29`)
- `app/controllers/reports_controller.rb:25` — `Issue.by_tracker/version/priority/category/assigned_to/author/subproject` (`:34-40`)
- `app/controllers/timelog_controller.rb` — time entries reference `issue` / `issue.visible?`
- `app/controllers/versions_controller.rb` — version "fixed issues" rollups
- `app/controllers/projects_controller.rb` — project show counts/destroys issues
- `app/controllers/repositories_controller.rb` — changeset→issue links
- `app/controllers/search_controller.rb` — searches issues via `acts_as_searchable`
- `app/controllers/queries_controller.rb` — runs `IssueQuery`
- `app/controllers/trackers_controller.rb` — tracker→issue counts
- `app/controllers/workflows_controller.rb` — status transitions for issues
- `app/controllers/custom_fields_controller.rb` — `IssueCustomField` management
- `app/controllers/users_controller.rb` — assigned-issue counts on user delete
- `app/controllers/previews_controller.rb` — previews issue notes/descriptions
- `app/controllers/application_controller.rb:394` — `find_issue` / `find_issues` (`:407`) shared loaders + visibility guard

### B. Models and associations
- `app/models/project.rb:40` — `has_many :issues, dependent: :destroy`; `:41` `issue_changes` through issues; `:336` issue-conditions SQL
- `app/models/time_entry.rb:25` — `belongs_to :issue`; `:66` `left_join_issue` with `Issue.visible_condition`; `:73` `on_issue`
- `app/models/journal.rb:112` — `journalized` (the issue); `:68` `visible` scope wraps `Issue.visible_condition`; `:127,165,186-205` visibility/notification delegation
- `app/models/issue_relation.rb` — `belongs_to issue_from/issue_to`, reschedule logic
- `app/models/changeset.rb:24` — `has_and_belongs_to_many :issues`; `:108-162` `scan_for_issues`, `fix_issue`, `log_time`; `:215` `find_referenced_issue_by_id`
- `app/models/version.rb:20` — `FixedIssuesExtension` (issue rollups, progress, est. hours)
- `app/models/user.rb:871` — notification dispatch `when Issue`; `:996-997` reassign author/assigned_to on user delete
- `app/models/group.rb` — group assignment of issues (assigned_to Principal)
- `app/models/member.rb` — member→issue assignment cleanup
- `app/models/tracker.rb` — `has_many issues`, disabled_core_fields drive `clear_disabled_fields`
- `app/models/issue_status.rb` — `update_done_ratio_from_issue_status`, open/closed
- `app/models/issue_priority.rb` — default priority assignment (`clear_disabled_fields`)
- `app/models/issue_category.rb` — `belongs_to issues`, reassignment on delete
- `app/models/issue_custom_field.rb`, `issue_priority_custom_field.rb` — custom field scoping
- `app/models/issue_import.rb` — bulk-creates issues
- `app/models/time_entry_import.rb` — imports time entries against issues
- `app/models/workflow_rule.rb` — status/field transition rules for issues
- `app/models/custom_field.rb` — `IssueCustomField` visibility/format
- `app/models/concerns/issue/webhookable.rb:20` — webhook payload assembly

### C. Reporting and queries
- `app/models/issue_query.rb` — primary issue query/columns/filters/stats SQL
- `app/models/query.rb` — base query referencing Issue columns
- `app/models/time_entry_query.rb` — joins to Issue columns
- `lib/redmine/helpers/time_report.rb:110-129` — groups time by issue status/version/category/tracker/issue
- `app/helpers/queries_helper.rb`, `timelog_helper.rb` — render issue columns/links

### D. Scheduling and rendering
- `lib/redmine/helpers/gantt.rb:159,168,182` — Gantt issues, relations, projects
- `lib/redmine/helpers/calendar.rb` / calendar views — issues placed by start/due dates
- `lib/redmine/export/pdf/issues_pdf_helper.rb` — PDF export of issues
- `app/helpers/issues_helper.rb` — `issue_list`, `render_issue_tooltip` (`:60`), descendants/relations stats (`:104-210`), spent/estimated/due details (`:257-297`), `issues_destroy_confirmation_message` (`:425`)
- `app/helpers/application_helper.rb` — `link_to_issue`, issue link parsing
- `app/helpers/my_helper.rb` — My-page issue blocks
- `lib/redmine/wiki_formatting/macros.rb` — issue-reference macros
- `lib/redmine/reaction.rb:23` — `REACTABLE_TYPES` includes `Issue`

### E. Integrations
- `app/jobs/webhook_job.rb:21` — async webhook delivery (issue payloads)
- `app/models/webhook.rb` / `webhook_payload.rb` — webhook config/payload
- `app/jobs/destroy_project_job.rb:48`, `destroy_projects_job.rb` — async cascade destroy of issues via project
- `app/models/mail_handler.rb` — inbound email creates/updates issues
- `lib/redmine/field_format.rb` — custom field formats render/validate on issues
- `lib/redmine/default_data/loader.rb` — seeds trackers/statuses/priorities issues depend on
- `lib/redmine/preparation.rb` — boot-time issue setup

### F. Notifications
- `app/models/mailer.rb:81` `issue_add`, `:105` `deliver_issue_add`, `:113` `issue_edit`, `:141` `deliver_issue_edit`
- `app/models/issue.rb:2103` `send_notification`, `:2096` `add_auto_watcher`
- `app/models/journal.rb:374` `send_notification`, `:186-205` `notified_users/watchers/mentions`
- `app/models/user.rb:863-891` — per-user notification eligibility for issues
- `lib/redmine/notifiable.rb` — notifiable event registry (issue_added/updated/etc.)

---

## Step 5 — How issue identity and visibility are guarded

**The two contract primitives** both live in `app/models/issue.rb`:
- `Issue.visible_condition(user, options)` (`:137`) — SQL fragment via `Project.allowed_to_condition(user, :view_issues)`, branching on `role.issues_visibility` (all/default/own), `is_private`, author/assignee, and per-tracker `view_issues` permission (`:159-166`)
- `Issue#visible?(usr)` (`:172`) — in-Ruby equivalent for a single loaded record

**Where the guards are applied per subsystem:**
- **Request entry:** `ApplicationController#find_issue` raises `Unauthorized unless @issue.visible?` (`application_controller.rb:398`); `find_issues` (`:407`) loads then filters

## Step 5 — How issue identity and visibility are guarded

**The two contract primitives** both live in `app/models/issue.rb`:
- `Issue.visible_condition(user, options)` (`:137`) — SQL fragment via `Project.allowed_to_condition(user, :view_issues)`, branching on `role.issues_visibility` (all/default/own), `is_private`, author/assignee, and per-tracker `view_issues` permission (`:159-166`)
- `Issue#visible?(usr)` (`:172`) — in-Ruby equivalent for a single loaded record

**Where the guards are applied per subsystem:**
- **Request entry:** `ApplicationController#find_issue` raises `Unauthorized unless @issue.visible?` (`application_controller.rb:398`); `find_issues` (`:407`) loads then filters
- **Background/project teardown:** `DestroyProjectJob` re-checks the actor is admin/active before destroying (`destroy_project_job.rb:16`); issues cascade through the already-authorized project
- **Reports/queries:** `Issue.visible` scope (`issue.rb:84`) joins project + `visible_condition`; `IssueQuery` builds on it; time reports join `Issue.visible_condition` (`time_entry.rb:70`)
- **Gantt/Calendar:** Gantt pulls `@query.issues` (`gantt.rb:159-160`) — the query applies the visible scope; projects limited to `Project.visible` (`gantt.rb:188`)
- **Email/notifications:** recipients filtered by `notified_users/notified_watchers/notified_mentions` (`issue.rb` / `journal.rb:186-205`); private-note recipients further filtered by `view_private_notes` (`journal.rb:189`); journal visibility delegates to `journalized.visible?` (`journal.rb:165-166`)
- **Autocomplete:** `Issue.cross_project_scope(@project, params[:scope]).visible` (`auto_completes_controller.rb:29`)
- **Time entries:** `belongs_to :issue` with `left_join_issue`/`on_issue` applying `visible_condition` (`time_entry.rb:66-73`); activity event only links issue `if o.issue.visible?` (`time_entry.rb:34`)
- **Journal detail rendering:** referenced-issue details gated by `Issue.find_by_id(...).try(:visible?, user)` (`journal.rb:127`)
- **Parent journaling under concurrency:** `Issue.visible.lock.find_by_id` (`issue.rb:2071,2081`)

**What they check:** project membership + role's `:view_issues` permission, the role's `issues_visibility` mode, `is_private` flag against author/assignee, and per-tracker view permission. Background paths re-establish actor identity (`User.current` / job user check) rather than trusting ambient state.

## Step 6 — Blast radius of the contract change (ranked by risk)

Changing **how the Issue stores and tears down attached data** (the four mixed dependent strategies in `issue.rb:41-46` plus reactions/attachments/custom_values).

**Critical (will page someone if missed):**
1. `app/models/issue.rb:124-134` — the destroy/after_save callback chain itself; reordering or changing dependent strategy breaks journal/parent rollup ordering (note the explicit ordering comments at `:127-131`)
2. `app/models/project.rb:40` — `has_many :issues, dependent: :destroy` cascades through every change; project teardown (`destroy_project_job.rb:48`) runs it async
3. `app/models/issue.rb:1845-1888` `recalculate_attributes_for` + `:2000` `reschedule_following_issues` — parent/child rollups on every save/destroy
4. `app/models/journal.rb:41-source` + `journal.rb:374` notification — journals are `dependent: :destroy`; losing them loses history and triggers/suppresses mail
5. `app/models/time_entry.rb:25` + `issues_controller.rb:436-460` — the destroy-time `destroy/nullify/reassign` choice depends precisely on the current `dependent: :destroy` on time_entries
6. `Redmine::NestedSet::IssueNestedSet` (`issue.rb:29`) — destroy reorders lft/rgt; concurrency-sensitive (`issue_nested_set_concurrency_test.rb`)

**High:**
7. `app/models/issue_relation.rb` (`issue.rb:45-46`, `delete_all`) — relations skip callbacks; reschedule/gantt depend on them (`gantt.rb:168`)
8. `app/models/changeset.rb:24,162` — HABTM join rows on destroy; `fix_issue`/`log_time` (`:154-155`)
9. `lib/redmine/reaction.rb:39` — reactions `delete_all` on destroy
10. acts_as_attachable (`issue.rb:48`) + `delete_selected_attachments` (`:126`) — orphaned files if teardown changes
11. `app/jobs/webhook_job.rb:21` + `Issue::Webhookable` — async payloads referencing torn-down associations
12. `Mailer.deliver_issue_add/edit` (`mailer.rb:105,141`, async `deliver_later`) — fire after commit; a destroyed/changed issue can be referenced post-teardown

**Medium:**
13. `IssueQuery`/`reports_controller.rb:34-40`/`time_report.rb` — stats SQL assuming current associations
14. acts_as_customizable custom_values (`issue.rb:49`) teardown
15. `version.rb:20` FixedIssuesExtension rollups
16. `user.rb:996-997`, `issue_category.rb`, `member.rb` — reassignment-on-delete paths

**Lower:** PDF/calendar/gantt rendering (`issues_pdf_helper.rb`, `calendar.rb`, `gantt.rb`), wiki macros, autocomplete — read-only, fail visibly not silently.

**Must re-verify after change:** destroy with time entries (all three `todo` modes), parent destroy cascading to children, project destroy job, reschedule cascade, journal+mail on create/update, webhook payloads, nested-set integrity under concurrency.
